metadata.title: Saša Pajić — Full Stack & AI Engineer
metadata.description: Full Stack Engineer and AI Developer building systems and tools that make work easier and more efficient.

content.headline: Functionality doesn't have to be ugly.
content.subheadline: I’m a Full Stack & AI Engineer who believes powerful backend logic deserves a beautiful frontend. It’s not enough for it to work—it has to feel right.

content.bio:
Most of my work starts with something that doesn't quite make sense yet. A half-formed idea, a system that's grown in strange directions, or a product that works but feels heavier than it should. I like sitting with that mess for a while and figuring out what's actually going on underneath it.

I'm patient with complexity, but I get uncomfortable with confusion. When something feels off, I usually want to trace it back to the decision that caused it and fix it there, instead of adding another layer on top. A lot of my time goes into small choices that won't show up in screenshots, but quietly shape how everything behaves later.

What keeps me interested is work where the outcome has weight. Not just shipping for the sake of shipping, but making something that holds together over time and doesn't treat the person using it like an afterthought. I like when a product has a clear direction, even if that means saying no to things.

Code I write with my head. Design I do with something less rational. When a layout clicks, it's not because I reasoned my way there — it's because something uncomfortable went quiet. I've learned to trust that feeling more than most rules.

I'm based in Valencia, Spain. Open to remote and B2B work.

items.title: S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ Platform
items.subtitle: Full-Stack SaaS — Psychological Safety
items.description: A self-service platform built from scratch for The Academy of Brain-based Leadership — delivering two flagship products digitally: the S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ psychometric assessment and PS Pulse™ team survey tool. Handles onboarding, assessment delivery, real-time scoring, instant PDF reports, payment processing, CRM sync, and an AI coaching feature.
highlights: Replaced a manual-first operation with a fully self-service product — onboarding, assessment, scoring, reports, and payment with no staff intervention required
Assessment completions increased 483% vs. prior year average following the platform launch
Instant client-side PDF report generation — full multi-page professional reports with no server queue or wait time
Payment webhook integration with zero-touch code provisioning and automatic CRM sync on every user action
Embedded AI coaching feature with real-time streaming and provider-agnostic model switching via environment variable
items.challenge:
ABL ran their two flagship products manually. There was no user-facing platform — coaches distributed access by hand, assessment results lived in a staff admin backend, and every new client meant more work for the same small team.

The brief was to build the product that didn't exist: a self-service platform where users could sign up, take the assessment, receive their report, and manage team surveys without anyone at ABL needing to get involved.
items.solution:
I built the full platform from scratch — onboarding with email verification, two-tier assessment delivery with access code validation, real-time percentile scoring, instant browser-side PDF reports, team survey management, a payment webhook integration, outbound CRM sync, and an embedded AI coaching feature.

The existing database of 3,500+ legacy assessments was absorbed into the new schema and retroactively scored, so the historical data remained accurate and useful from day one.

sections.title: Design Direction
sections.body:
ABL was specific about how the platform should look and feel. They didn't want a generic internal tool — they wanted something that reflected the quality of the work they do, and that their users could actually navigate without friction.

The UI leans dark with deliberate use of color: accents that direct attention without demanding it, plenty of breathing room around data, and typography that keeps scores and analytics readable at a glance regardless of how much is on screen.

Because the platform deals with sensitive psychometric data, clarity mattered more than cleverness. Every layout decision was about making the right information visible at the right time — nothing more.

sections.title: The Assessment
sections.body:
The S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ model is a neuroscience-based framework that measures how strongly a person responds to six psychological domains: Security, Autonomy, Fairness, Esteem, Trust, and You. Users complete 50 questions in 10–15 minutes, and the platform returns scores that reflect not just their answers — but where they stand relative to everyone who has taken the assessment before them.

The platform delivers this in two tiers. The free assessment gives users a basic view of their domain scores. The professional tier — unlocked with a single-use access code — produces a full multi-page report with detailed domain impact analysis and a personalized narrative. Codes are purchased directly through the platform or provided by a coach.

Scoring runs in real time against the full population of prior responses. Every submission is ranked, and the system is built to hold that speed well past 100,000 assessments without changes.

sections.title: PS Pulse™
sections.body:
PS Pulse™ is the team-level complement to the individual assessment. Where S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ gives each person a deep look at their own psychological profile, PS Pulse™ gives team leaders a recurring read on how their team is doing collectively.

Team leaders create named teams, generate per-team access codes, distribute a survey link, and receive aggregated domain scores on a dedicated dashboard. Survey cycles can be repeated — the platform tracks trends over time so leaders can see whether things are improving, stalling, or shifting.

Reports can be downloaded, emailed, or compared side-by-side against previous cycles directly in the dashboard. Assessment completions increased 483% vs. the prior year average after the platform launched — a direct reflection of how much easier the new experience made it to complete a cycle.

sections.title: Reports
sections.body:
Every professional S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ assessment produces a full multi-page PDF report — cover page, table of contents, scoring overview, per-domain charts, impact analysis, and a personalized narrative — generated entirely in the browser, instantly, with no server involved.

A user submits their assessment and the report is ready to download in the same moment. There's no queue, no wait, no backend infrastructure to maintain or scale. The report includes live data, so there's no lag between completing the assessment and receiving results.

The same applies to Pulse reports: team leaders can download or email a formatted PDF summary of any survey cycle directly from the dashboard.

sections.title: Payment & Integrations
sections.body:
Payments happen through ABL's existing CRM. When a purchase completes in Tekmatix, a webhook fires to the platform — which identifies the buyer, generates the correct access codes, saves them to the database, and emails a confirmation with the codes attached. No one at ABL touches anything. If the buyer doesn't have an account yet, the codes wait and link automatically on signup. Bundle purchases provision multiple codes in a single transaction.

Every significant user action — signup, assessment completion, Pulse activity — fires an outbound webhook to keep the CRM contact records current. ABL sees each user's full journey in their CRM timeline without any manual export step and without changing the way their team already works.

The platform also includes an AI coaching feature available to users as a preview: a streaming chat interface that responds in real time, with the underlying model swappable between providers via environment variable — no code change required.

responsibilities: Built and launched the S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ platform to measure and improve psychological safety across organizations
Developed PS Pulse™ as a continuous feedback and sentiment tracking system segmented by team and department
Implemented S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ assessment scoring logic, converting statistical models into algorithms producing raw scores and percentile rankings across multiple domains
Built client-side PDF report generation using @react-pdf/renderer for full multi-page professional reports
Integrated Tekmatix payment webhook for zero-touch access code provisioning and CRM contact sync
Built embedded AI coaching feature using LiteLLM with streaming SSE responses and provider-agnostic model switching
Implemented two-step email verification signup, profile completion gates, and HMAC-signed admin bridge API
Containerized frontend and backend with Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy with SSL termination on VPS
Implemented GitHub-based CI/CD pipelines to automate testing, builds, and deployments
Wrote automated tests for core business logic and data processing pipelines

items.title: ABL S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ AI Assistant
items.subtitle: AI/LLM Application — Content Generation
items.description: Built an AI-powered content platform enabling non-technical team members to generate blogs, email campaigns, social posts, and internal communications using guided prompts and persona-based controls.
highlights: Enabled non-technical team members to generate and iterate on on-brand content using guided prompts and persona-based controls
items.challenge: Non-technical staff needed to produce on-brand content across multiple formats (blogs, emails, social), but had no way to leverage AI tools without engineering support.
items.solution: Built a Streamlit app with persona-based customization, PDF Q&A with streaming responses, and integrated three separate AI providers (OpenAI via LlamaIndex, AWS Bedrock for Claude/DeepSeek, and direct Anthropic API). Used LangGraph for multi-step reasoning workflows.
responsibilities: Multi-provider AI integration (OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Anthropic)
Persona-based customization for role-specific outputs
PDF assistant with conversation history + streaming
LangGraph for complex multi-step AI workflows
Debug mode for system monitoring and model selection

items.title: RecOneAI
items.subtitle: AI Recruitment Automation Platform
items.description: Full-stack AI recruitment platform built for Totema — combining a production-ready FastAPI backend with a multi-page Streamlit UI. The system automates CV parsing, job description analysis, voice transcript processing, and candidate search via a conversational AI agent.
highlights: Delivered a full-stack AI recruitment platform as a solo project — production API backend + multi-page Streamlit UI
Applied multi-LLM routing: GPT-4o for classification, GPT-4o-mini for extraction, Claude Sonnet for vision, o3-mini for complex reasoning
Built TARA — a stateful conversational search agent with iterative query refinement before surfacing candidate results
items.challenge: Recruiters were manually reading hundreds of resumes and had no scalable way to process CVs, job descriptions, and voice call transcripts consistently or quickly.
items.solution: Designed a modular FastAPI + Streamlit platform: CV-to-JSON pipeline, JD parser, voice transcript classifier, CV optimizer, and TARA — an AI chatbot for natural language candidate search. Multi-LLM routing assigned each task to the best-fit model.
responsibilities: FastAPI REST API with token-based auth and multiple AI processing endpoints
CV-to-structured-JSON extraction pipeline with prompt engineering and output validation
Job Description parser converting raw text/PDF to structured filter criteria
Voice transcript classifier for extracting candidate identity data
CV Optimization module cross-analyzing CV vs. job description
TARA — conversational AI recruitment assistant with session-state management

items.title: Pragmatic Labs
items.subtitle: AI-First Enterprise Application
items.description: AI-first enterprise application focused on advanced prompt design and interaction workflows.
highlights: Building a “Deep Tech” visual style with a custom color scale and a geometric type system to make the product feel precise, trustworthy, and enterprise-ready.
Designing a chat interface that shifts its layout depending on what the AI is returning, so plain text and structured data are both easy to read and use.
Creating the Prompt Builder as a step-by-step flow with collapsible sections and example inputs to make complex configuration tasks easier to understand and complete.

items.title: CIMBA SAIGE
items.subtitle: Student AI Guidance Engine
items.description: Chat-based AI guidance tool designed to help university students navigate academic information and services.
highlights: Designed the user interface and component system with a focus on simple navigation and fast access to common student questions.
Created a chat-centered layout optimized for clarity and low cognitive load, making AI interactions easy to follow for non-technical users.
Migrated ~18,000 users and thousands of S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ assessment records from a legacy PHP system to a FastAPI/React platform with zero downtime
Assessment upgrades increased ~483% vs. prior year average following platform improvements
Engineered percentile-based scoring algorithm reducing computation complexity from O(N²) to O(log N), achieving 10–30ms processing and designed to scale to 100,000+ assessments
Optimized database operations via caching and indexing, reducing dashboard load times by 95% with sub-5ms query response times
Designed LLM-powered tools used by non-technical teams to generate and iterate on on-brand content
responsibilities: Built and launched the S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ platform to measure and improve psychological safety across organizations
Developed PS Pulse™ as a continuous feedback and sentiment tracking system segmented by team and department
Implemented S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ assessment scoring logic, converting statistical models into algorithms producing raw scores and percentile rankings across multiple domains
Built a Streamlit-based AI content assistant supporting blogs, email campaigns, social media posts, and internal communications
Built a RAG assistant allowing users to upload PDFs, ask questions, and maintain conversation history with real-time streaming responses
Integrated multiple AI providers: OpenAI (via LlamaIndex), AWS Bedrock (Claude, DeepSeek R1), and Anthropic Claude
Used LangGraph to support multi-step AI workflows and agentic reasoning
Implemented async email delivery and automated PDF report generation for high-volume assessments
Implemented GitHub-based CI/CD pipelines to automate testing, builds, and deployments
Wrote automated tests for core business logic and data processing pipelines

highlights: Delivered a full-stack AI recruitment platform combining a production-ready FastAPI backend with a multi-page Streamlit UI — solo project
Applied multi-LLM strategy: routed tasks to the best-fit model (GPT-4o for classification, GPT-4o-mini for extraction, Claude Sonnet for vision, o3-mini for complex reasoning)
Built TARA — a stateful conversational search agent that handles iterative query refinement before surfacing candidate results
responsibilities: Designed and developed RecOneAI, a multi-module AI-powered recruitment automation platform, from architecture through deployment
Built a FastAPI REST API with token-based authentication serving multiple AI processing endpoints
Integrated OpenAI GPT-4o and Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet via LangChain for document extraction, classification, and analysis
Implemented a CV-to-structured-JSON extraction pipeline with prompt engineering and programmatic output validation
Developed a Job Description parser converting raw text/PDF into structured candidate filter criteria
Built a voice transcript classifier extracting candidate identity data from unstructured call transcripts
Developed a CV Optimization module that cross-analyzes a CV against a job description and generates improvement suggestions
Built TARA — an AI-powered conversational recruitment assistant (Streamlit chatbot) with session-state management
Designed and built user interfaces for collaboration platforms supporting Cyber-Physical Systems development
Developed responsive, reusable UI components using HTML, CSS, TypeScript, and Angular
Improved usability and consistency across desktop and mobile devices

content.title: UI & Product Design

responsibilities: Built a "Deep Tech" visual style with a custom color scale and geometric type system to make the product feel precise, trustworthy, and enterprise-ready.
Designed a chat interface that shifts its layout depending on what the AI is returning, so plain text and structured data are both easy to scan and act on.
